name: Valdeganga along the banks of the Júcar river — circular from Jorquera
name_original: Valdeganga por la orilla del río Júcar — circular desde Jorquera
description: Cycle a difficult 36.5-mile loop along the Júcar River from Jorquera, featuring dramatic canyons and historic villages.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Jorquera offers routes through dramatic landscapes shaped by the Júcar River. The region features a spectacular gorge with cliffs up to 200 meters high, alongside varied terrain including olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields. Cyclists can explore barrancos and unique rock formations, with the town itself perched on a natural watchtower providing panoramic views. Best touring cycling routes around Jorquera The most popular touring cycling route is Casas de Juan Núñez, Jorquera y el río Júcar — circular desde Valdeganga, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Júcar River, offering views of its meanders and the surrounding agricultural landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ruta de Valdeganga - Las Mariquillas - Bolinches, a moderate 11.2 miles (18.0 km) path. This route traverses diverse Mediterranean flora and offers a dynamic backdrop for cyclists. Local touring cyclists also love the Valdeganga along the banks of the Júcar river — circular from Jorquera, a 36.5 miles (58.8 km) trail leading through the Júcar River gorge, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Jorquera is defined by the Júcar River gorge, high cliffs, and agricultural landscapes of olive groves and vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Jorquera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 8 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Jorquera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Jorquera?
A: The routes around Jorquera offer a diverse landscape. You'll primarily cycle along the dramatic Júcar River gorge, with cliffs reaching up to 200 meters high. The terrain also includes traditional Mediterranean flora such as olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields, providing a dynamic and scenic backdrop.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Jorquera?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there are options for less experienced cyclists. For instance, the region offers 13 moderate routes, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380344423/ruta-de-valdeganga-las-mariquillas-bolinches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de Valdeganga - Las Mariquillas - Bolinches&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.2 miles (18.0 km) long and traverses diverse Mediterranean flora.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes around Jorquera?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Jorquera offers 25 difficult routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063867412/valdeganga-along-the-banks-of-the-jucar-river-circular-from-jorquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeganga along the banks of the Júcar river — circular from Jorquera&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.5 miles (58.8 km) trail leading through the Júcar River gorge, typically completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I discover while cycling in Jorquera?
A: Jorquera is renowned for its spectacular natural beauty. You can expect to see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyons of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5971445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morrón – Júcar River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5972064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Azul Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Jorquera itself is perched atop a natural watchtower, offering incredible panoramic views of the surrounding area and the Júcar River's meanders.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions to visit along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore the historical town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/184407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá del Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, a Historic-Artistic site carved into rock, located just 16 km from Jorquera. The town of Jorquera itself boasts a 12th-century Almohad walled enclosure and its castle, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Jorquera?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Jorquera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380360544/casas-de-juan-nunez-jorquera-y-el-rio-jucar-circular-desde-valdeganga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas de Juan Núñez, Jorquera y el río Júcar — circular desde Valdeganga&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063867412/valdeganga-along-the-banks-of-the-jucar-river-circular-from-jorquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeganga along the banks of the Júcar river — circular from Jorquera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Jorquera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Jorquera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 5.0 stars from 8 reviews. Over 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the dramatic landscapes and the unique experience of cycling along the Júcar River.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Jorquera?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's Mediterranean climate suggests spring and autumn are ideal for touring cycling. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures, making it more comfortable to explore the varied terrain, olive groves, vineyards, and the Júcar River gorge.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near other villages?
A: Yes, cycling routes in the area often connect to nearby villages. For example, the municipalities of La Recueja and Cubas are within a short distance and offer more opportunities for nature and active tourism. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar – Alcalá del Júcar loop from Bormate&lt;/a&gt; will take you near Alcalá del Júcar.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring cycling routes in Jorquera?
A: The routes vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter, moderate paths like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380344423/ruta-de-valdeganga-las-mariquillas-bolinches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de Valdeganga - Las Mariquillas - Bolinches&lt;/a&gt; at 11.2 miles (18.0 km) taking around 1 hour 25 minutes, up to longer, more challenging rides such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar – Alcalá del Júcar loop from Bormate&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.1 miles (64.6 km) and can take over 4 hours.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Júcar River?
A: Many routes in Jorquera are centered around the Júcar River, offering continuous views of its meanders, gorge, and the unique rock formations it has sculpted. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6300733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Valley&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; are prominent features you'll encounter.

It is the typical town square surrounded by bars where locals socialize and cyclists take the opportunity to refuel and enjoy the sun on the terraces.

Pedaling between local orchards, on the east bank of the river, there are some ruins of a Gothic-style hermitage.


This section of the river is quite calm, with very little traffic as the road passes on the left side of the river.

From this area you can see a wide variety of birds of prey such as the golden eagle, the peregrine falcon or the eagle owl.
From the plateau at Alcozarejos it goes down to the Rio Jucar. Well-developed, low-traffic country road.
Asphalted path through the Júcar river gorge. We can observe the impressive rocky walls. Magnificent views.
On the hottest days, it is worth stopping at the town's river pool and taking a dip. Right next to it, there is a restaurant and a large grove where you can rest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is quite varied. While some descriptions suggest mostly paved surfaces, others indicate significant unpaved sections, including dirt tracks that might be challenging for traditional touring bicycles. You should be prepared for a mix of asphalt, paved sections, and potentially rougher unpaved paths, especially if you are on a touring bike. A gravel or mountain bike might offer a more comfortable and capable experience for the varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is officially graded as 'difficult' for touring cycling, with an elevation gain of over 600 meters. While some sections might be manageable, the overall difficulty and the potential for unpaved, challenging terrain suggest it requires good fitness and some cycling experience. It's not typically recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Jorquera. You can typically find parking within or near the village of Jorquera to begin your ride. Specific parking facilities are not detailed, but Jorquera is a small town where street parking or designated village parking areas should be available.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 58.8 kilometers long and is estimated to take around 4 hours and 17 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle. This duration can vary depending on your fitness level, the type of bike you're using, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Júcar River route?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning natural beauty, characterized by dramatic canyons, towering limestone cliffs, and lush riverbanks carved by the Júcar River. The route offers spectacular viewpoints as the river winds through gorges, and you'll pass through charming settlements like Jorquera, a historic village perched on a rock.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several points of interest. You'll pass by a Riverside recreation area, the Town Hall Square in Jorquera, and an Hermitage. The natural highlights include the impressive Canyons of the Júcar River, the Júcar River Gorge Trail, and the Jorquera River Pool.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular cycling route are not provided. Generally, dogs are allowed on many trails in Spain, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Júcar River area can experience hot summers. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for cycling, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Winter can also be an option, but be prepared for cooler weather and potentially shorter daylight hours.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that special permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route along the Júcar River. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near charming settlements like Jorquera. You can expect to find local cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options in Jorquera and other nearby villages, which are ideal for a break or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the 'Sendero del Rio Jucar' and 'Las ramblas de la Abadia y el Batán'. You'll also find smaller portions overlapping with trails like 'Cumbres y barrancos Hoz del Júcar' and 'Camino de Valdeganga a Bolinches'.
